title: Deterministic automation authority
summary: Agents MUST use declared repository automation for deterministic operations and MUST NOT reproduce the same operation manually.
instructions: When the active invocation declares an automation requirement, the agent MUST invoke the named automation against the declared target with the declared arguments before performing the affected operation.

Agents MUST treat durable repository automation as authoritative over ad hoc shell sequences or per-run scripts and MUST NOT manually recreate its behavior unless the requirement declares an explicit fallback.

When no durable automation exists, an agent MAY create a task-specific script under runtime/logs/workflows/<run-id>/scripts/ only for the current run and MUST test its core behavior and likely edge cases before relying on it.

A missing executable, non-zero exit, timeout, malformed result, or target mismatch MUST be reported as an automation failure and MUST follow the requirement's declared failure route; agents MUST NOT bypass the failure by asserting an equivalent manual result.
The exact command, target, result, and evidence path MUST be recorded for every required automation invocation.
Harness-owned operations and gates MUST remain harness-owned even when an agent runs the same automation for early feedback.
